Title: Alternaria TeA toxin activates a chloroplast retrograde signaling pathway to facilitate JA-dependent pathogenicity

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes foliar lesion development in this pathosystem
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic SA depletion line; RT-qPCR; JA measurement; lesion phenotyping; chlorophyll fluorescence; SOSG imaging
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SA depletion by NahG enhances SORG expression, JA accumulation, and lesion development after AaWT infection without increasing singlet oxygen levels
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NahG-mediated SA depletion enhances AaWT-induced lesion development. 2. SA depletion increases JA synthesis and JA-responsive outputs. 3. The result supports antagonism between SA and JA and reinforces a positive JA–singlet oxygen feedback loop.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SA depletion may compromise SA-dependent immunity; this study focused on increased necrotrophic disease symptoms
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a transgenic metabolic perturbation tool rather than an endogenous Arabidopsis gene; included because it is a core experimental factor directly altering hormone metabolic balance

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Chloroplast JA precursor formation from α-linolenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates α-linolenic acid in the chloroplast JA pathway upstream of OPDA formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes necrotrophic foliar cell death and disease development in this pathosystem
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Alternaria alternata wild-type inoculation; TeA-dependent singlet oxygen signaling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly induced after AaWT infection in an EX1-dependent manner
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; lesion phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: lox3 mutants showed reduced foliar lesion development and impaired induction of EX1-dependent singlet oxygen-responsive genes after AaWT infection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX3 is a JA biosynthetic gene rapidly induced after AaWT infection. 2. Its induction depends on TeA and EX1/2-mediated singlet oxygen signaling. 3. Loss of LOX3 reduces disease lesion formation, supporting JA as a downstream effector of EX1/2 signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced JA synthesis is associated with reduced necrotrophic disease development in this study; broader defense tradeoffs not analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modulating LOX3 or upstream EX1-TeA signaling could reduce JA-supported necrotrophic lesion development, but possible impacts on defense and wound signaling should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as one of several JA-pathway mutants to connect EX1/2-dependent singlet oxygen signaling to downstream JA biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Chloroplast conversion toward allene oxide/OPDA branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: fatty acid hydroperoxides derived from α-linolenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: unstable allene oxide precursor of OPDA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 13-hydroperoxy linolenic acid-derived substrate to an allene oxide en route to OPDA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes foliar lesion development and JA signaling during Alternaria infection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AaWT infection; TeA-dependent singlet oxygen signaling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after AaWT infection; rescued by EX1-GFP in ex1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in ex1, EX1-GFP ex1, and Col-0
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AOS expression is induced downstream of EX1 during infection, supporting increased JA synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOS is among JA synthesis genes upregulated during AaWT infection. 2. This induction requires EX1-mediated singlet oxygen retrograde signaling. 3. Restoring EX1 rescues AOS induction together with JA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering node for altering pathogen-induced JA production, though broad JA-related defense effects may occur
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No dedicated aos mutant disease phenotype was tested in the main figures; evidence is transcript-level and pathway-context support

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes the unstable allene oxide to form OPDA in the chloroplast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AaWT infection; TeA-triggered singlet oxygen signaling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly induced after AaWT infection in an EX1-dependent manner
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; JA measurement; SOSG imaging; lesion phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: aoc3 mutants showed reduced lesion formation, failed JA synthesis, and strongly impaired SORG induction despite normal singlet oxygen accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC3 is induced by AaWT in a TeA- and EX1/2-dependent manner. 2. aoc3 mutants are compromised in JA synthesis and show reduced disease development. 3. Comparable singlet oxygen but reduced SORG induction in aoc3 supports a positive feedback loop between JA and singlet oxygen signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower JA can attenuate lesion development but may alter defense outputs more broadly
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: AOC3 is a candidate target to uncouple pathogen-triggered singlet oxygen from JA-amplified disease development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper specifically used aoc3 to show that reduced SORG expression is not due to reduced singlet oxygen levels

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Peroxisomal reduction step from OPDA toward JA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: OPC-8:0 (reduced OPDA derivative en route to JA)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces OPDA in the JA biosynthetic pathway leading to jasmonic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes foliar lesion development during Alternaria infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after AaWT infection; reduced in ex1 and rescued in EX1-GFP ex1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; lesion phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: opr3 mutants exhibited reduced lesion development and impaired induction of EX1-dependent SORGs after AaWT infection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPR3 is transcriptionally induced during AaWT infection in an EX1-dependent manner. 2. Genetic loss of OPR3 compromises disease lesion development. 3. The data support JA production downstream of EX1-mediated singlet oxygen signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be targeted to reduce JA-supported necrotrophic disease symptoms, with caution for broader JA-related functions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes opr3 is deficient in both JA and JA-Ile

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates JA to isoleucine to form bioactive JA-Ile
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to disease-promoting JA signaling during Alternaria infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; lesion phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: jar1 mutants showed reduced foliar lesion development and reduced induction of EX1-dependent SORGs after AaWT infection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JA-Ile synthesis contributes to AaWT-induced lesion development. 2. jar1 mutants support a role for bioactive JA conjugate signaling in disease promotion. 3. Reduced SORG induction in jar1 suggests positive feedback between JA signaling and singlet oxygen-responsive outputs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A candidate node to attenuate JA-Ile-mediated susceptibility, although pleiotropic effects on JA signaling are likely
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper distinguishes JA and JA-Ile contributions using opr3 and jar1 mutants

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: chloroplast singlet oxygen sensor and retrograde signaling regulator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: EXECUTER family; SOS domain containing protein with critical W643 residue
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Retrograde signaling linking chloroplast singlet oxygen to jasmonic acid synthesis/signaling and cell death
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: singlet oxygen sensing followed by signaling activation associated with EX1 proteolysis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SORGs including AAA+-ATPase; SIB1; WRKY33; WRKY40; JA biosynthetic genes LOX3; AOS; AOC3; OPR3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates expression of singlet oxygen-responsive genes and promotes induction of JA synthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: TeA toxin-derived chloroplast singlet oxygen; AaWT infection
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with EX2 in EX1/2-mediated singlet oxygen retrograde signaling; FtsH protease discussed as implicated in EX1 turnover in prior work
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream sensor connecting TeA-induced singlet oxygen to JA accumulation and disease-promoting signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; complementation with 35S:EX1-GFP; domain deletion and W643 substitution lines; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; JA quantification; lesion phenotyping; western blot
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of EX1 compromises SORG expression, JA synthesis, and foliar lesion development after AaWT infection; EX1-GFP restores these phenotypes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EX1 is required for TeA-triggered chloroplast-to-nucleus singlet oxygen signaling during Alternaria infection. 2. EX1 acts upstream of JA synthesis and JA-responsive gene induction. 3. The SOS domain and W643 residue are essential for pathogenicity-associated signaling and lesion formation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes necrotrophic foliar cell death and disease development in this pathosystem
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AaWT versus AaMU inoculation; exogenous TeA; continuous light; high light plus cold acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: positive feedback with JA signaling is supported because JA-deficient mutants show reduced SORG induction
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced EX1 signaling lowers disease lesions in this necrotrophic interaction; broader acclimation and stress-signaling tradeoffs are likely
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in complementation and EX1 variant lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally active in mature leaves during infection; transcript pattern not emphasized
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA PRJNA640476; MS/PTM analysis of EX1 peptides
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: EX1 singlet oxygen sensing module is a potential engineering target to reduce TeA-facilitated disease, but may affect chloroplast stress acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TeA triggers rapid EX1 degradation. No nuclear relocalization was detected under TeA treatment in this study. W643 oxidation was not significantly increased by MS despite functional requirement of W643.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: EX1/2-mediated chloroplast singlet oxygen retrograde signaling upstream of JA synthesis/signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive regulator of singlet oxygen-responsive retrograde signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SORG program and JA-related gene induction at pathway level
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes induction of singlet oxygen-responsive and JA-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: TeA-derived chloroplast singlet oxygen; AaWT infection
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts with EX1 in EX1/2-mediated signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required together with EX1 for full TeA-dependent induction of JA-related genes and disease lesions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double mutant analysis; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; JA quantification; lesion phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ex1/2 plants show impaired SORG expression, reduced JA accumulation, and reduced foliar disease development despite similar singlet oxygen levels
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EX2 participates with EX1 in TeA-triggered singlet oxygen signaling. 2. EX1/2 are required for full induction of JA-related genes and JA accumulation. 3. Loss of EX1/2 suppresses lesion development without reducing singlet oxygen production.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: positive feedback with JA inferred at pathway level
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely affects broader singlet oxygen acclimation and stress responses
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential susceptibility regulator target, though pleiotropic chloroplast stress effects need caution
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EX2 was mainly assessed as a partner with EX1 in the ex1/2 double mutant rather than independently
